    5. Journaling: The Art Of Collecting Your Truth
</h2>

<p></p><p><span style="color: var(--squid-ink);">If you already know WHY journaling is important-- skip to the bottom for:</span></p><p><strong>HOW TO JOURNAL FOR TRANSFORMATION</strong></p><p><br></p><p></p>

<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p><span style="color: var(--squid-ink);">You've heard it a million times:</span></p><p>You should journal.</p><p>And for some reason, you still don't do it.</p><p>"I'm not a writer." </p><p>"I'll remember. I don't need to write it down."</p><p>"I tried. But I fell off..."</p><p>You say you want to succeed at any cost? And that you were "made for this"? But then you're too lazy to put all the chances on your side by doing the MOST BASIC thing that speeds up the journey? </p><p>Tell me... Especially if you have big goals... Like, G.O.A.T. level goals...</p><p>How will you know how you're doing?</p><p>Especially on the days when you feel disillusioned?</p><p>Are you going to rely on your memory?</p><p>What did you do for your career last Thursday? And the Monday before that? How does this July compare with July last year with your sleep habits?</p><p>Uh-huh... Your memory ain't so good now is it?</p><p>Your diary is your memory.</p><p>"I don't need to write it down," is laziness parading as bravado.</p><p>Your mind doesn't WANT to remember.</p><p>People like to remember their past as worse or better than it was. </p><p>When you're feeling down, you think you always sucked. </p><p>And when you're on cloud nine... Everything goes through rose-colored glasses.</p><p>This is fun if you're writing a script or a novel... But it's not helpful for self-improvement or for your mental health. </p><p>That's why you keep a journal. To see your journey without the influence of your current emotions.</p><p>Get rid of the "filters". Start building the next version of yourself swiftly. Analyze your journey without the influence of your current emotions.</p><p>Face the numbers. Face the data.</p><p><br></p><h2>KNOW YOUR TRENDS</h2><p>Creatives are like athletes:</p><p>We need to keep track of our progress when we've put ourselves on a path to change the world.</p><p>We're emotional people, but emotions deceive. That's why we keep a journal as an insurance policy against that deception.</p><p>People seek help every time they're at the same point of their cycle of doubt.</p><p>But they don't even realize it's a cycle. They don't even realize that it's the same point every time because they don't journal.</p><p>Without a journal, you keep learning the same lessons over and over again.</p><p>Why not build on the wisdom you've accumulated instead?</p><p>Know your trends.</p><p>As student Mayra says:</p><p>"We shouldn't be fighting our biology. <span style="color: rgb(34, 34, 34);"> My menstrual cycle and natural hormonal changes influences my mental state and creative output. Knowing that it's normal prevents me from entering a downward mental spiral. Instead... I can plan my work around it. I can plan a different kind of TODO list created for exactly those days. And progress can continue..."</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(34, 34, 34);">And boys? You have a biological cycle too. Use journaling to help you figure it out.</span></p><p><br></p><h2>JOURNALING FOR MENTAL HEALTH</h2><p>So, you're thinking about getting meds for your anxiety? Maybe that's the right solution for you. But have you TRIED a solution that's worked for millions of people throughout the ages?</p><p>Try it.</p><p>It's available without a prescription.</p><p>I'll show you how to do it in a fun, addictive way.</p><p>Wanna get out of those dark times quicker?</p><p>Write it all down.</p><p>"There's something magical about writing things down."</p><p>The philosopher Wittgenstein stated it first. At least that's what someone told me when I told him that I've been keeping a diary since I was 14.</p><p>Wittgenstein discovered that when you put your thoughts down on paper... It's much easier to throw them away.</p><p>It's like finishing a jigsaw puzzle.</p><p>It's like, over. Done.</p><p>That's what writing your thoughts down does.</p><p>Seriously. Have you ever notice how many G.O.A.Ts talk about keeping a journal?</p><p>But they never tell you why.</p><p>They say "softball" things like it's "for gratitude".</p><p>Well, if you're the "heated yoga" type of person, then I guess that's a good enough answer.</p><p>But for the rest of us, Nah.</p><p>This why journaling is powerful:</p><p>So that you can see your trends.</p><p>It's essential to be able to read about all the times you ridiculously thought "your life was over"... But somehow, you overcame.</p><p>A journal is a testament to what you've been through AND survived.</p><p>And the way I will teach you to journal in this module, you will be able to finally use that "data" to your benefit.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>HOW TO JOURNAL FOR TRANSFORMATION - STEP ONE: ESTABLISH YOUR FRAME</strong></h1><p>Before you start journaling, you'll need a frame for this new experience. </p><p>Write these two things down:</p><p><br></p><h2>1. Write down your "Polaris Star".</h2><p><br></p><h2>2. Write down a 3 sentence "game plan" to get there.</h2><p><br></p><p><strong>YOU HAVE TO BE ABLE TO SEE THESE TWO STATEMENTS EVERY SINGLE DAY YOU WAKE UP. OTHERWISE, YOU'LL FORGET THEM. AND BELIEVE ME, 'THEY' WANT YOU TO FORGET THEM. 'THEY' DON'T WANT TO BE SHAMED WITH YOUR INCREDIBLE WORK ETHIC.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>Now for the fun part:</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>HOW TO JOURNAL FOR TRANSFORMATION - STEP TWO: KEEP A TRACKING JOURNAL</strong></h1><p>Your new journal is about to become be the un-edited novel of how you achieved your goals.</p><p>A "tracking journal" is a special type of journaling.</p><p>It's "data entry" so that you can discover your "truths" (more on that later).</p><p>It's the "creative athlete's" alternative to a diary-style journal.</p><p>The basic concept is this:</p><p>Imagine you went to the gym three times a week, but you didn't keep track of how many sets, reps, the order and time it took. You would take longer than everyone else to progress.</p><p>Creatives are like athletes in that regard.</p><p>And the bigger the goals, the more intense the documentation of your progress has to be. </p><p>But remember</p><p>Journaling isn't writing. We don't care about spelling or punctuation.</p><p>You're not trying to win a literary award. </p><p>Your journal can be all point form entries if you want.</p><p>Or diagrams.</p><p>Or lists of ideas.</p><p>You can even put it in the form of a spreadsheet if you like (how I do it half the time). </p><p>Here are a few ways people do it:</p><h2>Get a pretty book.</h2><p>This is the most common type for a reason. It's easy, and it works.</p><p>OR...</p><h2>Start an excel spreadsheet. </h2><p>Perfect for you if you don't wanna be touchy-feely about it. (Whenever I'm about to undergo an intense transformation, <a href="https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1R09l5gLgnLHzoJsI4hiybcPDaJL7puGuBSNFTYGLDHU/edit?usp=sharing" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">I move my journaling to a Google docs spreadsheet. </a>This enables me to see all the data every day at one glance.)</p><p>OR...</p><h2>Buy a large calendar (aka The Jerry Seinfeld Method, look it up)</h2><p>OR...</p><h2>Create a post-it notes bulletin wall in a corner of your home. (Like Eric Andre does on set.)</h2><p>Look... It doesn't matter what you choose.</p><p>You're creative. Be creative with it.</p><p>The only thing that matters is that you make it something that YOU'LL enjoy going to every day.</p><p>And don't worry... It'll get sexier the moment you start seeing results.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>HOW TO JOURNAL FOR TRANSFORMATION - STEP THREE: WHAT TO WRITE</strong></h1><p>Here are the things you need to document every day in a tracking journal (you can write more, but these are key):</p><h2>1. What time you wake up.</h2><h2>2. What time you go to bed (which you will write down for the night before)</h2><h2>3. How you feel.</h2><h2>4. What you learned today.</h2><p>(Wait 'till you see what you learn just from these 4 data points over a few weeks. It's astounding.)</p><p>Other things people like to document based on their personal goals:</p><p>What did you eat today? (Dancers and musicians especially. You'd be surprised how much food plays into your moods and productivity.)</p><p>What did you buy (like Warhol did in The Andy Warhol Diary at the end of every entry. You'll also be able to keep track of if you suffer "shiny object syndrome" or "too many mentors".)</p><p>But the journaling game-changer is this last step:</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>HOW TO JOURNAL FOR TRANSFORMATION - STEP FOUR: KEEP A LIBRARY OF YOUR 'TRUTHS'</strong></h1><p>In the back of your journal, start collecting your TRUTHS.</p><p>Start a new page with this title in bold: MY TRUTHS. </p><p>The "MY TRUTHS" section is a collection of the indisputable lessons that you will figure out along the way.</p><p>They are the "cannot-miss" learnings that will become part of your personal bible. (One day you'll be able to turn them into your own Mystery School). </p><p>When you collect enough of these TRUTHS... You stop feeling like a passenger in your own life.</p><p>You start navigating new situations as if you've already been down that road before. Your past experiences become metaphors for your unique challenges.</p><p>Most people only develop their "truths" through the hard lessons of life or by extreme trauma.</p><p>What a horrible and painful way to learn.</p><p>And so slow.</p><p>And it leaves you bitter.</p><p>This Mystery School ' tracking journal' will get you there faster and with grace. </p><p>By continually taking inventory of who you are along the way... And who you are becoming... You know that challenges are only plot-twists in your story.</p><p>Journaling for others is "writing things down". </p><p>For you, it's the documentation of creating A LIFE THAT'S A WORK OF ART.</p><p>It's where you put the subtle touches on your "personal culture".</p><p>Soon you will "Know thyself."</p><p>After months of journal tracking, you will re-read sections and see how much of a legend you are.</p><p>You will read about moments where you thought life was over and see how you got out of those messes in vivid detail.</p><p>And THAT'S what will reinforce your memory.</p><p>Your Mystery School journal will flow like a script for an action-thriller-comedy.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>Dear Student... </strong></h1><p>There are two moments in your life when you will "figure it all out":  </p><p>1. When you have the perfect window to apply it.</p><p>Or...</p><p>2. When it's too late.</p><p>Start today.</p><p>To be the best version of yourself requires courage.</p><p>How do you get that confidence?</p><p>By knowing yourself.</p><p>How do you get to "know thyself"? </p><p><strong><em>1. By keeping a journal and learning your trends.</em></strong></p><p><strong><em>2. By converting those trends into your TRUTHS. </em></strong></p><p><em>Identify your strengths. This will give you confidence.</em></p><p><em>Understand your weaknesses. This will give you wisdom.</em></p><p>Start tracking your progress right now even if you feel like you aren't doing much.</p><p>That's how every movie starts. With the protagonist "not doing much".</p><p>Just before their greatest adventure.</p><p>Isn't it time to document yours?</p><p><a href="https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1R09l5gLgnLHzoJsI4hiybcPDaJL7puGuBSNFTYGLDHU/edit?usp=sharing" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Here is a GOOGLE SPREADSHEET you can download to get you started. (It's what I use when I need a military-style focus.)</a></p><p></p>
</div>

Slowly I started doing it throughout the day, always having my journal with me wherever I went. I still had/have difficulty remembering to write down the essentials, since I mostly focused on writing about big emotions which I felt were important for the future. I also kept a backlog of truths in the back, and it's interesting to see what you can learn from your daily life only by analyzing yourself and what you do, and draw conclusions from your daily life. Sleep is essential. Fat makes me tired. Eating well and cooking is important. Some basic stuff. But also learnt that if I have a lot of things on my mind, I start doing things more carelessly.

      <div data-original-comment-body="the trick is to not write in my journal as if any one is gonna read it. that's when i get to the real shit. but after a few years in a narcissistic abusive (family) relationship where there were real invasions to my privacy, real manipulations of anything personal this woman got about me, and she actually stole my damn journal out of my drawer and read it, there's some very real trauma around my sacred lil leather-bound book. 

tryin to reclaim my words, my personal thoughts, all that goodness and realness BACK for mycellf, now that i've had to cut that person who has become sadly toxic, outta my life. 

sharing this not for advice, or as an excuse, or whatever else anyone wants to assume... I mention it cuz it feels important to say that writing in journal YES or writing in journal NO aren't the only experiences of journaling, and that it can be hard to reclaim that place after trauma around it but it ain't impossible and it's so fucking worth it!!! &lt;3" class="comments__block-box__body">the trick is to not write in my journal as if any one is gonna read it. that's when i get to the real shit. but after a few years in a narcissistic abusive (family) relationship where there were real invasions to my privacy, real manipulations of anything personal this woman got about me, and she actually stole my damn journal out of my drawer and read it, there's some very real trauma around my sacred lil leather-bound book. 

tryin to reclaim my words, my personal thoughts, all that goodness and realness BACK for mycellf, now that i've had to cut that person who has become sadly toxic, outta my life. 

sharing this not for advice, or as an excuse, or whatever else anyone wants to assume... I mention it cuz it feels important to say that writing in journal YES or writing in journal NO aren't the only experiences of journaling, and that it can be hard to reclaim that place after trauma around it but it ain't impossible and it's so fucking worth it!!! <3</div>
